mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, leading to positive SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are not followed by positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate by more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes negative corporate earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 45% of observations over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate a broad-based labor market recovery that boosts consumer income expectations and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE decline and PAYEMS increase episodes fail to generate positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is constrained at the zero lower bound, month-over-month industrial production growth becomes the primary driver of equity returns because monetary policy cannot provide additional stimulus, so positive production surprises drive positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes do not produce statistically significant positive 60d returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals capacity utilization improvement and margin expansion for industrial sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal FEDFUNDS is stable but CPIAUCSL accelerates, deeply negative real interest rates push investors into equities and drive positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint stable FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL episodes fail to generate positive 5d returns in > 55% of cases over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals construction sector strength and upstream demand for materials and labor, transmitting into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in > 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Funds rate increase of more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 6-month window signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered tightening episodes produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 45% of observations over any 3-year rolling period.
